reasoning: The Lean signature is a direct, faithful encoding of the informal statement. "Homomorphism of G onto G'" is captured by `φ : G →* G'` together with `hφ : Function.Surjective φ`. "N ⊴ G" is captured by `N : Subgroup G` with the `[N.Normal]` instance hypothesis. The conclusion "φ(N) ⊴ G'" is captured by `(Subgroup.map φ N).Normal`, where `Subgroup.map φ N` is the image of N under φ. The groups G and G' are arbitrary (Type* with Group instances), matching the generality of the informal claim. This exactly mirrors Mathlib's own `Subgroup.Normal.map` theorem in `Algebra/Group/Subgroup/Basic.lean`.
